note: Not a near-tangency with the close-pass circle. The two dense integrations, from bit-identical initial conditions, separate smoothly (roundoff-level at t=10) and then grow ~exponentially (e-folding time about 18.6 time units, fit t in [20,180]) until their separation exceeds the close-pass threshold itself by t=219 -- 109 time units before the tight integration's own genuine near-collision with Jupiter (r2=0.00010, 204.0x inside the threshold) at t=287.5. At that exact instant the baseline integration is at r2=1.1864, nowhere near Jupiter: it is simply no longer following the same trajectory, chaos having amplified its coarser tolerance past the point where "same orbit" is still a meaningful description.
